Awareness - of the need for change: Unknown: may not know a portal exists or why it might help them
Desire - to participate in change: Low: currently familiar with calling agents but may prefer web portals for convenience - there is an assumption that delayed / missed manual follow-ups are preventing debt collections but no hard evidence
Knowledge - how to change: Low: Have no direct training on using the portal, although many users may be familiar with online payment / banking systems
Ability - to implement change: Unknown: varying levels of digital ability assumed but no evidence
Reinforcement - to sustain the change: Low: A Legacy-Trust-led change will offer the opportunity for all customers to use a web portal, but there is no enforcement

Awareness - of the need for change: Medium-high: aware of the time and stress to consolidate records in order to serve customers, lots of time spent on low-complexity cases
Desire - to participate in change: Medium: likely frustrated by time pressure and work stress, but little direct evidence to support this
Knowledge - how to change: Medium-high: full training will be provided, and experience over time will provide confidence, but exception-case handling may take time to understand well
Ability - to implement change: Medium-high: full training will be provided, workflow has been mapped, but agent skill with portal will take time to develop
Reinforcement - to sustain the change: Low (high-risk): agents may find workarounds as is the current practice

Awareness - of the need for change: High: project stakeholders, with clear evidence of awareness of operational pain points
Desire - to participate in change: High: With clear process map and justified recommendations, in favour of operational efficiencies - though with concerns about creating more work or ambiguity
Knowledge - how to change: Medium-high: training will be provided; there is a time-cost to developing familiarity with the portal; managing agents and the new workflows may take some time to develop confidence in
Ability - to implement change: Medium: assumed competency in management and familiarity with team, but no direct evidence on ability to navigate the change well
Reinforcement - to sustain the change: Medium: KPI alignment will motivate change management, but these are assumed and not evidenced

Awareness - of the need for change: High: Danile Okoye is aware of revenue leakage and likely operation inefficiencies too
Desire - to participate in change: High: with justified economic and operational case (ROI), in favour of recovering lost leakage and improving workflows
Knowledge - how to change: Low: likely little awareness of operational pain points or experience of practical causes of revenue leakage; has a more strategic, high-level view of the business
Ability - to implement change: High: budget-holder and policy-setting authority, will approve (or gain approval from board) on any strategic change
Reinforcement - to sustain the change: Medium: revenue recovery will motivate and validate value of change, but no formal process (apparently) to reinforce it
